title: Player appearance is twelve equipment slots, five body colors, skull/prayer icons, and movement sequences.
implementationRule: The browser actor state must decode the same raw appearance packet fields and carry them into player model composition before player appearance parity is considered implemented.

title: Player models are composed from kit/item model parts, recolored, cached, then sequence transformed.
requiredSnippets: public Model method4156(SequenceDefinition primarySeq, int var2, SequenceDefinition var3, int var4)
implementationRule: Do not render a player until the cache exporter can produce equipment model parts and the runtime can compose them in this slot order.

title: Client model geometry exposes vertices, triangle indices, face colors, alpha, priorities, and textures.
implementationRule: The GLB/exported mesh format must preserve these fields or an explicitly equivalent representation.

title: Model frame transform type 5 adjusts triangle alpha labels and clamps the byte alpha range.
implementationRule: Sequence playback must apply type-5 frame transforms to exported face alpha groups rather than treating alpha-only frames as no-ops.

title: The cache model export carries both base face alpha and triangle skin labels for alpha transforms.
implementationRule: Spotanim and model GLB metadata must preserve faceSkins alongside faceAlphas so frame transform type 5 can select the same triangle alpha labels as the client.

title: Sequence definitions use frame ids and frame lengths over OSRS frame-map transforms.
implementationRule: Animation playback must consume exported sequence/frame-map transforms; do not invent skeletal animation timing.

title: Primary actor sequences combine with movement sequences through the primary sequence interleave mask.

implementationRule: When a player has both a primary action and movement sequence, the trainer must apply the primary sequence with the movement sequence using the primary sequence interleave labels.

implementationRule: Dual actor sequence playback must apply primary transforms outside the interleave set, movement transforms inside it, and type-0 transforms in both passes.

title: The render sequence export carries cache interleave labels needed for dual sequence playback.
implementationRule: Named render sequence fixtures must preserve interleaveLeave so runtime actor playback can mirror SequenceDefinition.applyTransformations.

title: Actor movement can be stalled by active primary sequences through precedenceAnimating and priority.
implementationRule: Manual trainer movement must consult exported sequence precedence/priority metadata and allow primary actions such as crossbow or barrage to stall visible/path movement when the client would.

title: Sequence precedenceAnimating and priority default from interleave labels when not explicitly encoded.
implementationRule: The trainer must post-decode sequence movement metadata the same way the client does: explicit values win, otherwise interleaved sequences default to 2 and non-interleaved sequences default to 0.

title: Target-facing uses exact actor coordinate deltas rather than eight-way path facing.
implementationRule: Attack/follow facing must use exact actor-to-target deltas, while tile path movement can keep eight-way step-facing.

title: Actor turning keeps orientation and rotation separate and only uses turn movement from ready movement.
implementationRule: Manual trainer turning must update visible rotation gradually from desired orientation and only substitute the source turn movement sequence when the current movement is the actor ready sequence.

title: Model drawing rotates source-local vertices before applying actor translation.
requiredSnippets: public final void method2373(int var1, int var2, int var3, int var4, int var5, int var6, int var7, int var8) {
implementationRule: Actor model wrappers must preserve the source model X/Z origin and rotate the root there; do not recenter player meshes around their bounding-box center before yaw.

title: Player models apply primary action sequence first and use movement sequence when it differs from ready or when no primary action exists.
requiredSnippets: Model var4 = this.appearance.method4156(var2, super.sequenceFrame, var3, super.movementFrame);
implementationRule: Replay actor sequence selection should prefer active primary actions, otherwise use movement when it differs from ready/pose before falling back to pose.

title: Actor movement sequences loop, while primary action sequences advance by frame lengths and terminate or loop via frameCount/maxLoops.
implementationRule: Renderer sequence sampling should loop pose/movement frames but must not wrap primary action sequences forever when the source sequence terminates.

title: Projectile visual motion derives speed, yaw, pitch, frame cycle, and acceleration from source/target/cycle fields.
implementationRule: Projectile renderer must be driven by Kronos projectile payloads and client motion math.

title: Spot animations apply their sequence frame before scale and orientation transforms.
implementationRule: Projectile and actor graphics must resolve cache spotanim ids to their sequence ids, then apply exported frame-map transforms to the spotanim mesh.

title: Server projectile packets provide gfx id, source/target tiles, target index, heights, delay, duration, and curve.

startHeight, endHeight, delay, duration, curve, idk);
implementationRule: The trainer event bus should carry this payload shape rather than renderer-specific projectile shortcuts.

title: Target spells emit animation, cast graphics, optional projectile, delayed magic hit, block graphics, hit graphics, and XP.
implementationRule: Magic simulation and rendering must be event-driven from the same spell cast contract.

title: Ice Barrage uses Kronos spell ids, gfx ids, projectile id, max hit, and freeze duration.
requiredSnippets: public void afterHit(Hit hit, Entity target) {
implementationRule: Barrage visual and freeze behavior must match these ids and the after-hit hold duration before NH mage parity is accepted.

title: Food and karambwan locks are distinct and directly delay combat in different ways.
implementationRule: Supply actions must model eat, karam, and potion delays separately; one generic food cooldown is not acceptable.

title: Consumable item actions remove food, replace potion doses, and turn emptied potions into vials.

implementationRule: Clickable trainer inventory supplies must visibly mutate the widget slot the same way Kronos item actions mutate Item ids instead of only recording a local click event.

title: Granite maul specials queue through PlayerCombat, auto-attack after one tick, drain 50 percent each, and expire.
implementationRule: Spec timing must mirror the queue/auto-attack path, including delayed client control cases.

title: NH bot melee reach accounts for same-tile under, cardinal adjacency, frozen diagonal failure, and one-tick step-in reach.
requiredSnippets: private boolean canMeleeReachThisTick(Player attacker, Player defender)
implementationRule: Melee opportunity metrics and pathing must use this contract or a tested equivalent.

title: Server tick length has live and training profiles.
implementationRule: The standalone trainer must represent tick duration explicitly and avoid hard-coding one global rate.

title: Core worker processes game events before online players.
implementationRule: The trainer tick runner should keep game-event processing separate from per-player processing.

title: Player processing order separates hits, events, updates, pre-attack, target route, movement, attack, prayer, and stats.
implementationRule: Do not merge movement, attack, prayer, and stat updates into one unordered simulation step.

title: Hits are defended, queued, and processed separately from direct HP deltas.
implementationRule: Damage reward and render timing must read actual hit events, not infer damage from HP changes after healing.

title: Hit client delays convert projectile timing into server ticks before accuracy and damage finalization.
requiredSnippets: public Hit clientDelay(int delay, int cycleRate) {
implementationRule: The trainer must carry client delay and resolved damage as separate facts.

title: Movement consumes queued steps against the entity position each tick.
implementationRule: Movement intent must be modeled as queued tile steps, not arbitrary world-position interpolation.

public static boolean withinDistance(Entity entity, Entity target, int distance) {

title: Combat attack delay tracks last attack tick plus additive food/potion attack delays.
implementationRule: Supply actions must add to attack delay rather than overwrite weapon cooldown.

title: Player combat dispatches magic, ranged, melee, and Gmaul only after target route reach checks.
implementationRule: The standalone combat dispatcher must preserve the same route gate and style ordering.

title: Ranged attacks use weapon ticks, rapid style reduction, max hit, special handling, and projectile graphics.
implementationRule: Ranged EV and timing must know attack ticks, rapid style, max hit, spec interception, and projectile timing.

title: Attack bonus calculations branch by magic, range, and melee attack styles.
requiredSnippets: public static double getAttackBonus(Entity entity, AttackStyle attackStyle, AttackType attackType) {
implementationRule: Style EV must be derived from real attack/defence formulas and equipment bonuses.

title: Hit chance compares attack and defence rolls using Kronos' branch formula.
implementationRule: Comparative style EV must use this branch formula after deriving attack and defence rolls.

title: Weapon type definitions contain attack distance, ticks, animations, sounds, styles, and render animations.
implementationRule: Do not hard-code whip/crossbow/kodai timing; import weapon type metadata.

title: Item bonus JSON order includes attack, defence, strength, damage, prayer, and weight fields.
implementationRule: Equipment EV must use numeric bonuses rather than item-name shortcuts.

title: Equipment recomputes aggregate bonuses from worn items.
implementationRule: Trainer gear stats should aggregate from item definitions exactly once per equipped state.

title: Prayer definitions include overhead icons, level requirements, drain, and stat boosts.
implementationRule: Prayer UI, damage reduction, and offensive boosts must share one imported prayer table.

title: Player prayer activation enforces requirements, disallowed groups, active flags, drain, and boosts.

implementationRule: The trainer should expose prayer choices and drain through the same group/boost constraints.

title: Kronos prayer tab child actions toggle Prayer.ordinal()+5 widgets.
implementationRule: The trainer prayer tab must dispatch normal prayer icon clicks by source child id and Prayer ordinal rather than browser-only button ids.

title: Protection prayers reduce player-versus-player damage to sixty percent for the matching style.
implementationRule: Defensive prayer reward must evaluate actual on-prayer/off-prayer damage with client-visible timing.

title: Equipment switches remove the selected inventory item and swap the worn item back into that same container slot.
implementationRule: Visible gear switches must mutate fixed inventory slots by swapping against currently worn equipment, not by hiding equipped items from a static template.

title: Container packets populate inventory widget item ids and quantities slot-by-slot.
implementationRule: Runtime inventory snapshots must represent the same widget itemId/itemQuantity slot updates the client receives from container packets.

title: Client stat and run-energy state is stored in current level arrays and runEnergy from server packets.
implementationRule: Fixed-mode HUD values must be driven by client-visible stat and run-energy snapshots rather than hardcoded browser text.

title: Widget CS1 value evaluation reads current levels, real levels, experience, varps, run energy, and weight.
implementationRule: Browser HUD text should consume explicit client-view values equivalent to the widget CS1 sources instead of static local constants.

title: Saradomin brew boosts HP and defence while draining attack, strength, ranged, and magic.
implementationRule: Brew-down EV must use boosted/drained visible stat levels, not fixed base 99 stats.

title: Granite maul special performs an immediate extra attack, animation, gfx, sound, and drains fifty percent.
implementationRule: Gmaul use/success/failure should be an explicit event the AI can observe audibly/visually after it happens.

title: NH bot run loop captures tick, heartbeat, food/potion flags, freeze route reset, and death state.
implementationRule: The standalone bot loop should expose the same per-tick facts before policy decisions.
